Defendant Debra Schoenberg, Schoenberg Family Law Group, P.C., Tari Quinn, Quinn Takarada P.C. Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Helbraun, Esq. 415-982-40000 of Helbraun Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Peter Weber, Esq. 415-788-1900 of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The court shares his views. Defendant Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Debra Schoenberg, Schoenberg Family Law Group, P.C., Tari Quinn, Quinn Takarada P.C. Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Defendants Debra Schoenberg and Schoenberg Family Law Group, P.C.'s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. The motion is solely based on the questionable proposition of law that an attorney who negligently performed services for a client during the course of a litigated case is not liable for her negligent conduct because the attorney substituted out of the case and another attorney took over the representation of the client with sufficient time to remedy the negligence of the first attorney. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Continued To Dec-15-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement And Dismissal Of Cross-Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 877.6
Off Calendar Jun-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER L. WEBER OF MURPHY PEARSON BRADLEY & FEENEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-788-1900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
ATTY. DAVID S. McMONIGLE OF LONG & LEVIT LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-438-4555 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT ALBERT J. RASCH.

RULING - Judge Murphy disclosed that he was co-counsel with James Murphy a couple of years ago and shares office space with David Helbraun. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Yunqui "Mindy" Chan's motion for determination of good faith settlement and dismissal of cross-complaint is GRANTED. "The party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue." (CCP 877.6(d).) In this case, substantial evidence supports the finding that the settlement between Ms. Chan and Mr. Rasch is in good faith under Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. A rough approximation of Ms. Chan's recovery is $1,000,000. Mr. Rasch's declaration shows that his main role in the underlying case was to examine the couple's finances and Ms. Schoenberg and Ms. Quinn were the family law lawyers who took the lead in litigating the divorce proceedings. Mr. Rasch was not present the day that the divorce proceedings settled and the gravamen of Ms. Chan's case is that the attorneys committed malpractice by approving of that settlement. Mr. Rasch correctly notes that a party settling should pay less in settlement and the settlement for $143,000 ($70,000 from insurance and waiver of $73,000 in fees) is in the ballpark. There is no evidence of collusion and Mr. Rasch demonstrates that he does not have substantial assets to independently pay off a judgment that Ms. Chan may recover. The request for further briefing is Denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-20-2016 Set For Trial On Jun-05-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Allen Kuo of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (415-788-1900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS DEBRA SCHOENBERG, SCHOENBERG FAMILY LAW GROUP, P.C., TARI QUINN and QUINN TAKARADA P.C.'S Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint against Albert Rasch is GRANTED. Defendants have shown good cause for their request and it is supported by California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. Plaintiff's principal arguments essentially contend that defendants waived their right to seek to bring Mr. Rasch into this case after plaintiff dismissed him. No act or omission of defendants is in any way suggestive of such a waiver. Nor has plaintiff shown how she would be prejudiced by granting the motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
